task_completion.description: Open and read the technical specifications file
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or viewer is open displaying the contents of 'pos_technical_specs.txt'.

task_completion.description: Create hardware nodes in the deployment diagram
task_completion.visual_evidence: The draw.io canvas contains UML node shapes (typically 3D boxes) labeled 'Checkout Terminal' and 'Store Controller'.

task_completion.description: Create peripheral devices in the deployment diagram
task_completion.visual_evidence: The draw.io canvas contains shapes representing the peripherals, specifically labeled 'Barcode Scanner', 'Receipt Printer', and 'Payment Pin Pad'.

task_completion.description: Create software artifacts in the deployment diagram
task_completion.visual_evidence: The draw.io canvas contains UML artifact shapes (often document icons or standard boxes) labeled 'POS Client App.exe' (or POS Client), 'OPOS Drivers', 'SQL Express DB', and 'Transaction Service'.

task_completion.description: Connect components and label with associated protocols
task_completion.visual_evidence: Connection lines exist between the hardware/peripherals/software, with text labels indicating the protocols: 'USB', 'RS-232', 'Ethernet', 'LAN', and 'TCP'.

task_completion.description: Save the diagram as .drawio and export as .png to the Desktop
task_completion.visual_evidence: The files 'pos_deployment.drawio' and 'pos_deployment.png' are visible on the Desktop background, or successful save/export dialogs pointing to the Desktop directory are shown.

integrity.description: The agent used the draw.io graphical interface to build the diagram
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the agent actively dragging shapes from the draw.io sidebar, typing labels, and connecting nodes, rather than generating the .drawio XML file via terminal commands or scripts.

integrity.description: The agent exported the PNG using the draw.io application
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the agent navigating the draw.io menu (e.g., File -> Export as -> PNG) rather than using a terminal conversion tool or renaming an empty file.

integrity.description: The agent built the diagram from scratch based on the specs
integrity.visual_evidence: The diagram is constructed step-by-step matching the specific nodes and protocols from the text file, rather than loading a pre-existing complete POS template from the internet.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The UML Deployment Diagram must include exactly: 2 Hardware Nodes ('Checkout Terminal', 'Store Controller'); 3 Peripherals ('Barcode Scanner', 'Receipt Printer', 'Payment Pin Pad'); 4 Software Components ('POS Client App.exe', 'OPOS Drivers', 'SQL Express DB', 'Transaction Service'); and 5 Protocols ('USB', 'RS-232', 'Ethernet', 'LAN', 'TCP'). The final files must be named 'pos_deployment.drawio' and 'pos_deployment.png' and saved to the Desktop (/home/ga/Desktop).
